attacking_style: Opportunistic sprinter from reduced groups rather than pure power sprinter. Showed willingness to contest intermediate points even within breakaways, indicating tactical awareness beyond stage wins.
team_tactics: Limited data, but appears to operate with autonomy in breakaways, making independent tactical decisions (KOM competition vs. cooperation) suggesting freedom or low team support.
terrain_preference: Reduced bunch sprints after selective racing; capable in breakaways but not a pure climber. Suits stages where attrition creates smaller finishing groups.
vulnerability: Likely exposed in pure mass sprints against top-tier sprinters. Breakaway tactics suggest preference for avoiding direct confrontation with lead-out trains.
key_patterns: Contests secondary objectives (KOM points) even when in breakaways, showing multi-goal racing approach.
Wins from reduced groups after late catches, suggesting strong finishing speed in smaller fields.
Operates in breakaways tactically, not purely as escape artist but calculating positioning.
form_trajectory: positive: Stage win and active tactical racing within same Grand Tour stage race indicates good condition
